document.nameEN: Insulated-gate bipolar transistor (IGBT) power module PRIVATE 2
document.productDescription: One Click LCA generic material. The reference product is silicon IGBT power module (traditional power module with DBC/DBC substrate and baseplate). This product is commonly used for high-frequency power conversion in inverters, drives and other power converters. The bill of materials and manufacturing energy has been generated with AI assisted methods, enabling efficient data modelling in the absence of specific sourcing information. Designed to provide a practical and representative baseline, it is particularly useful during early stage assessments when no locally applicable manufacturer specific profiles are available. The dataset has passed the standard quality assurance process including benchmarking against available industry product data to ensure accuracy, relevance, and credibility. Basic manufacturing principle is (1) manufacture of a ceramic-copper bonded substrate (DBC/DCB) including ceramic forming/sintering and copper patterning/etching, (2) injection molding of polymer housing parts, (3) soldering/attach of semiconductor dies and terminals to the substrate, (4) wire bonding for electrical interconnects, and (5) final assembly and electrical test, with the key physical/chemical transformations explained. Average technique used in industrial electronics manufacturing (global / EU-type supply chains). Main constituents are copper, DBC substrate (alumina + copper), glass-fiber reinforced PPS plastics, silicone gel encapsulant, steel fasteners, lead-free Sn-Ag-Cu solder, doped silicon dies. The dataset is representing unpackaged product and no materials and energy for packaging are included. Process level additional waste streams and fugitive emissions are included using primary data where available or the defaults documented in the instructions.
document.staticFullName: Insulated-gate bipolar transistor (IGBT) power module PRIVATE 2 (One Click LCA)
document.upstreamDB: One Click LCA, Plastics Europe, IDEMAT, ProBas

document.environmentDataSource: EPD SPIDI MAX WALL BRACKET (EN-5052, A2 1.4301, S250GD) From SLAVONIA BAUBEDARF GMBH
document.nameEN: Galvanised steel  wall brackets for ventilated facades
document.pcr: The CEN standard EN 15804 serves as the core PCR. In addition, the RTS PCR (English version, 12.11.24) is used
document.productDescription: The patented SPIDI® max wall brackets are ETA-approved and available in lengths from 40 mm to 900 mm. They are made of aluminum, steel with additional cathodic dip coating for enhanced corrosion protection and stainless steel A2. The stainless steel brackets are also passive house certified. All brackets have a 6 mm pre-assembled thermostop, can be used as a fixed and sliding point and can be installed both horizontally and vertically. Further information can be found at https://slavonia.com/en/.
document.staticFullName: Galvanised steel  wall brackets for ventilated facades, 0.6430 kg/unit, SPIDI® max (Slavonia Baubedarf GmbH)

document.environmentDataSource: EPD 1 meter steel reinforced fundation Pile in S2 Concrete 35 x 35 type 20 with 20 rebar
document.pcr: NPCR Part A:2021 ,NPCR 020 Part B for Concrete and Concrete Elements
document.productDescription: A precast pile is a long, slender structural element made of reinforced concrete, manufactured off-site and driven into the ground to transfer loads from a structure to deeper, more stable soil layers. These piles are pre-engineered for high strength and durability, making them ideal for projects requiring deep foundation systems.
document.staticFullName: Precast steel-reinforced concrete piles, 35X35 mm, S2, 301.24 kg/m, 2318 kg/m3 (Centrum Pæle A/S)

document.pcr: NPCR 009:2021 Part B for Technical - Chemical products for building and construction industry
document.productDescription: Information on the technical data of StoPox KU 611 as well as the mixing and application process can be found on the Technical Data Sheet. StoPox KU 611 is an epoxy coating in accordance with EN 1504-2 and  EN 13813 for interior application with a gloss appearance. It can be used as a coloured, electrically conductive coating for industrial flooring and for surfaces with high mechanical and chemical stress, e.g. HBV systems and ESD protection zones. StoPox KU 611 is  a component of StoFloor Cleanroom system 3 and StoFloor ESD KU 611. It is electrically conductive in accordance with EN 1081 and EN 61340-4-1. StoPox Ku 611 has to be prepared and mixed with a stirrer and applied manually in accordance with the technical data sheet.  StoPox KU 611 is delivered in two components, StoPox KU 611 Component A and StoPox KU 611 Component B, which have to be mixed as stated on the Technical Data Sheet before use. This EPD calculates the environmental impact of the correctly mixed components at the construction site.  The composition of the mixed product is stated in the following table:
document.staticFullName: Epoxy coating  for industrial flooring, 1470 - 1570 kg/m3, StoPox KU 611 (Sto SE & Co. KGaA)

document.nameEN: Concrete admixture to reduce the amount of water
document.pcr: NPCR 009:2021 Part B for Technical - Chemical products for building and construction industry
document.productDescription: Dynamon NRG-670 is an advancement of Mapei’s Dynamon SP system. The Dynamon SP system is based on DPP technology (Designed Performance Polymer), a chemical process where the admixtures´ properties are custom-made for different concrete uses. This process has been developed with help from Mapei’s own composition and production of monomers. Concrete with Dynamon NRG-670 added gives good workability (Consistency Class S4 or S5, based on EN 206), making pouring easier. At the same time, it gives concrete excellent mechanical properties when finally cured and hardened.  Dynamon NRG-670 consists of a new generation acrylic-based polymers. The polymers are quite effective in dispersing clusters of cement grains and ensuring speedy temperature rise inside the concrete. This effect is primarily used most advantageously in the following three ways: - To reduce the amount of water at the same workability. - To increase workability when water/cement ratios are equal. - To reduce both water and cement when water/ cement conditions and workability are equal. Dynamon NRG-670 is a modified acrylic-based polymer blend, manufactured especially for the element industry.  Dynamon NRG-670 is particularly well suited in the manufacture of elements where considerable water reduction and high early strength is needed within the many different consistency classes. Dynamon NRG-670 is ideal for production of self-compacting concrete, as it ensures good flow and accelerated solidity development.  It  is available in 25 liter cans, 200 liter drums, 1000 liter IBC tanks and in tank.  For more information see the TDS (Technical Data Sheet) on Mapei AS website (www.mapei.com/no).
document.staticFullName: Concrete admixture to reduce the amount of water, Dynamon NRG670 (Mapei S.p.A)

document.pcr: NPCR Part A:2021, NPCR 020 Part B for Concrete and Concrete Elements
document.productDescription: Concrete is a building material made up of several components, including cement, water, sand, gravel, and air. Ready-mix concrete is manufactured in a batch plant in a controlled environment, using precise mix designs (with the addition of other cementitious materials or chemical admixtures that improve the properties of the concrete), ensuring consistency in quality, strength, and composition. This consistency leads to predictable performance in construction projects. Ready-mix concrete is then delivered to the construction site in an unhardened state, ready to use, eliminating the need for on-site mixing. This saves time in labor, equipment setup, and material handling, speeding up the construction process. The product is produced according to DIN EN 206. Testing was conducted according to EN 12350 and EN 12390. Performance data of the product with respect to its characteristics in accordance with the relevant technical provision (no CE-marking).
document.staticFullName: Ready-mix concrete, C30, 2400 kg/m3, STD Concrete no admix (Sika Services AG)

document.commercialName: CPGBox83500 with side length 35 cm and 8 locks
document.environmentDataSource: EPD CPGBox83500 with side length 35 cm and 8 locks
document.pcr: NPCR Part A:2021 , NPCR 013 Part B for Steel and Aluminium Construction Products
document.productDescription: Steel is a versatile alloy composed primarily of iron and carbon, valued for its exceptional strength, durability, and adaptability across a wide range of applications. Available in multiple grades and finishes—including carbon steel, stainless steel, and alloy steels—it offers varying levels of hardness, corrosion resistance, and weldability. With consistent mechanical properties and high tensile strength, steel serves as a foundational material in construction, manufacturing, and infrastructure. Steel is widely used in structural frameworks for buildings and bridges, automotive components, pipelines, heavy machinery, and consumer appliances. In construction, structural steel beams and columns form the skeleton of high-rise buildings and industrial facilities. In manufacturing, steel is machined into precision parts or rolled into sheets for appliances and enclosures. Its ability to be welded, formed, and treated makes it suitable for demanding environments requiring strength, reliability, and longevity.
document.staticFullName: Foundation pile connection box, 60.9 kg /unit, 7850 kg/m3, CPGBox83500 with side length 35 cm and 8 locks (Centrum Pæle A/S)
